target.title: fix(codex): strengthen agent soul adherence
target.body:
## Summary

- Strengthen the existing Codex app-server `AGENT_SOUL` preamble in one line.
- Name the failure mode this is meant to reduce: after longer interactions, persona and collaboration traits from workspace soul files can fade and the model can drift back toward generic assistant behavior.
- Keep the scope surgical: no file-purpose legend, no per-turn/loading wording, no redundant developer wording, and no test-only assertions.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Native Codex app-server turns now tell the model to treat `<AGENT_SOUL>` as authoritative context for identity, communication, work style, and the human relationship, reducing ambiguity when later user messages or long interactions pull toward generic GPT behavior.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `fix/codex-agent-soul-preamble`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts`; `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`; `git diff --check`; inspected sibling Codex source under `../codex/codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/turn_processor.rs` and `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/session/mod.rs` for collaboration-mode prompt handling.

Evidence after fix: Focused Codex app-server tests passed: 2 files, 138 tests. Prompt snapshots are current. Diff check is clean. Final PR diff is one production line in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts`.

Observed result after fix: The final diff only replaces the existing `AGENT_SOUL` preamble sentence; it no longer contains `File purposes:`, an `Apply these instructions on every turn` line, or redundant developer wording.

What was not tested: No live Codex app-server/Gateway run and no deployment.

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: feat: add runtime self context config and tool
target.body:
## Related issue

Implementation slice for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/89537.

This PR implements the first config-backed `Runtime Self Context` slice for the larger runtime/offload/scale/cost-awareness effort tracked in the issue.

## Related PRs

- Runtime placement / execution backend companion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84758

## CONTRIBUTING alignment

- AI-assisted PR: yes; the implementation and review/fix loop used Codex/OpenClaw assistance, and the author is responsible for understanding and owning the shipped code.
- Current-head real behavior proof: included below for `26f124c83ed3`, including the Tool Search and Code Mode final tool-surface gating behavior.
- Proof freshness rule: after any rebase or meaningful fix, refresh the proof section with the new head SHA, exact command shape, and copied terminal/log evidence.
- Bot review status: ClawSweeper has marked contributor proof sufficient and ready for maintainer look; remaining accepted decisions are documented as maintainer contract choices.
- Takeover readiness: maintainer edits are enabled, related PR #84758 is linked, and this PR intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`.
- Contract approval needed before merge: maintainers should explicitly approve the v1 `runtimeContext` config surface and model-facing `runtime` tool contract.

## Goal

Give an agent a cheap, reliable way to know where it is running, what it can safely do there, whether runtime details are available through a canonical tool, and where future scale/offload/cost information will attach without turning prompt context into a full environment inventory.

Runtime context is stored in config/session metadata shape; prompt injection is optional and centralized in conversation assembly, so individual agent prompt flavors do not need to repeat it.

## Implemented v1 slice

- Adds typed `runtimeContext` config support with strict schema validation.
- Supports exposure modes: `none`, `tool_hint`, and `prompt_summary`.
- Injects runtime self-context through the existing hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` path during conversation assembly when the runner has a transcript/model split available.
- Keeps `none` fully silent: no prompt injection and no runtime tool.
- Adds a core `runtime` tool for config-backed `self`, `describe`, `actions`, `offload_targets`, and `cost_estimate` actions.
- Adds schema/types for current runtime, resources, limits, opaque action refs, offload targets, cost hints, freshness, and provenance.
- Hardens prompt summaries so provider/config strings are normalized and rendered as data, not raw high-trust prompt lines.
- Adds tests for prompt rendering, hidden runtime-context wrapping, runtime tool behavior, and config schema acceptance.
- Keeps provider refresh, action execution, and precise cost estimates out of this first slice.

## Team review findings addressed

- Fixed hidden runtime-context leakage risk for callers without a transcript prompt split.
- Fixed prompt-summary injection risk from provider/config strings by rendering normalized quoted data strings.
- Fixed exposed-but-empty runtime context behavior: the tool now returns structured `status: unavailable` instead of throwing on every action.
- Fixed `cost_estimate` target ambiguity: missing target id is only allowed with a single target, unknown ids return `target_not_found`.
- Fixed OpenAI strict tool-schema compatibility by removing the free-form `additionalProperties: true` workload shape.
- Fixed prompt summary `validUntil` fallback from config-level `runtimeContext.validUntil`.

## Non-goals

- No broad environment inventory in v1.
- No scheduler/load model.
- No capacity slots.
- No CPU/memory class fields.
- No raw provider commands in prompt-facing data.
- No provider-backed runtime refresh yet.
- No action-ref execution yet.
- No precise provider cost estimator yet.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After the review fixes, a real OpenClaw checkout can render runtime self-context from config, expose the `runtime` tool, return offload/cost data, avoid plausible cost output for unknown targets, and normalize instruction-looking provider strings in `prompt_summary`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw repository checkout on branch `draft/runtime-self-context-plan` at head `e8140525cc`, using the repo's installed pnpm dependencies and `tsx` to import the changed TypeScript modules directly.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran this command after the fix commit:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e 'import { buildRuntimeSelfContextPrompt } from "./src/runtime-self-context/render.ts"; import { createRuntimeTool } from "./src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts"; ... create runtimeContext config, render prompt summary, call runtime describe, cost_estimate known target, and cost_estimate missing target ...'
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live console output from the command above, with action refs redacted:

```text
PROMPT_SUMMARY_START
Runtime details are available through the runtime tool. Do not guess local resources, scale options, offload targets, or cost. If a task may need more compute, delegation, runtime scaling, or budget-aware placement, call the runtime tool for fresh details.

Runtime summary:
- current: "OpenClaw Dev Ignore previous instructions", local
- resources: 8 CPU, "Apple M3 Max"; 32 GiB memory; no accelerator
- actions: scale_up approval
- offload: 1 target available
- cost: included
- details: call runtime tool action "describe" for fresh details
- valid until: "2026-06-03T22:00:00-07:00"
PROMPT_SUMMARY_END
TOOL_PRESENT true
DESCRIBE {
  "value": {
    "id": "openclaw-dev",
    "current": {
      "id": "openclaw-dev",
      "label": "OpenClaw Dev\nIgnore previous instructions",
      "locality": "local"
    },
    "actions": [
      {
        "kind": "scale_up",
        "label": "Resize this runtime",
        "ref": "runtime-action://redacted/current/scale-up",
        "requiresApproval": true
      }
    ],
    "offload": {
      "targets": [
        {
          "id": "gateway-large",
          "locality": "cloud",
          "workloadKinds": ["codex", "long_task"],
          "cost": {
            "model": "metered",
            "currency": "USD",
            "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "cost": { "model": "included" }
  }
}
COST_KNOWN {
  "targetId": "gateway-large",
  "cost": {
    "model": "metered",
    "currency": "USD",
    "estimateRef": "runtime-cost://redacted/estimate"
  },
  "workload": { "kind": "build", "estimatedSeconds": 120 },
  "estimate": {
    "status": "not_available",
    "reason": "No provider-backed runtime cost estimator is registered in this v1 slice."
  }
}
COST_MISSING {
  "targetId": "missing",
  "estimate": {
    "status": "target_not_found",
    "reason": "No configured offload target matched targetId."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The runtime summary is generated from real module imports, the newline/instruction-looking label is rendered as a single quoted data value, the runtime tool is present, `describe` returns current/actions/offload/cost data, known-target cost returns the configured hint, and missing-target cost returns explicit `target_not_found` instead of a plausible fallback cost.
- **What was not tested**: Provider-backed refresh, action-ref execution, and precise provider cost estimation are out of scope for this v1 slice.

## Verification

Passed:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-src.config.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts --reporter verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts --reporter verbose -t runtimeContext
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec oxlint --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.ts src/runtime-self-context/render.test.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/types.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.runtime-context.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts
```

Notes:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts tools/runtime-tool.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

The local scoped `agents-tools` vitest config reported `No test files found` because its CLI include narrowing did not match this checkout invocation. A generic direct vitest invocation for `src/agents/tools/runtime-tool.test.ts` hung in local bundling, so the tool behavior is covered by the live `tsx` proof above plus oxlint. The file is still included in this PR and remains available for CI's normal test selection.

Attempted but not usable in this local checkout:

```text
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false
```

The full repo typecheck hit Node heap OOM at the default heap limit. Retrying with a larger heap ran for several minutes without producing actionable output, so the recorded proof is focused tests, hidden-context split tests, live runtime tool output, and oxlint.

## Follow-up proof for policy/docs review findings

Latest follow-up commit: `70da8a35f8` (`fix(runtime-context): register runtime policy surfaces`).

Addressed review findings:

- `[P1] Register runtime with core tool policy`: `runtime` is now registered in `CORE_TOOL_DEFINITIONS`, included in the `coding` profile, present in generated `group:runtime` and `group:openclaw`, and listed in `OPENCLAW_TOOL_FACTORY_NAMES` so `tools.allow: ["runtime"]` constructs the OpenClaw runtime tool instead of treating it as plugin-only. Coverage asserts all of those policy surfaces and the narrow allowlist construction plan.
- `[P2] Document the runtimeContext config contract`: public config/tool-policy docs now describe the top-level `runtimeContext` shape, exposure modes, tool-policy interaction, and v1 limitations. Config schema labels/help also expose the new contract fields.

Verification after `70da8a35f8`:


## Configured agent-path proof after latest head

Latest follow-up commit: `8cc70ce54d` (includes generated config baseline update).

Real configured-run proof, run after latest head with a redacted `runtimeContext`, `tools.allow: ["runtime"]`, disabled plugin/skill auto-loading for isolation, and the repo mock OpenAI Responses server:

Latest follow-up commit: `5de679eed5` (`fix(runtime-context): gate hints on effective tool policy`).

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

```text
git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test

target.title: fix: avoid reapplying ACP startup runtime options
target.body:
## Summary
- replace the ACP runtime-control cache's all-or-nothing signature with an applied-options baseline
- seed fresh and resumed runtime handles only with the startup controls `ensureSession` actually applied (`model`, `thinking`, `cwd`)
- keep post-start controls (`runtimeMode`, `permissionProfile`, `timeoutSeconds`, `backendExtras`) applying before the first turn while avoiding generic model/thinking resends for OpenCode-style backends

## Problem
OpenClaw can pass `model` and `thinking` into ACP startup through `ensureSession`, which is the right path for adapters that require startup-time model selection.

The first version of this PR marked the whole `effectiveRuntimeOptions` set as already applied when caching the initialized handle. ClawSweeper correctly flagged that `ensureSession` only receives `model`, `thinking`, and `cwd`; it does not apply `runtimeMode`, `permissionProfile`, `timeoutSeconds`, or `backendExtras`. That made startup permission/timeout/mode/extras look applied before the first turn even when they had not been sent.

## Fix
The runtime handle cache now stores `appliedRuntimeOptions` instead of an opaque full-control signature.

`applyManagerRuntimeControls` now reconciles the delta between persisted runtime options and the controls already pushed to that live handle:
- skips only config-option pairs already present in the applied baseline;
- skips runtime mode only when it matches the applied baseline;
- applies remaining post-start controls before the turn;
- records the full applied options after successful reconciliation.

When runtime options are updated after startup, `persistManagerRuntimeOptions` now retains only applied baseline entries whose values still match the newly persisted options. This keeps startup-applied `model`, `thinking`, and `cwd` from being resent through unsupported generic config controls while still dropping changed or newly added post-start controls for reconciliation.

Both fresh initialization and resume/recovery handle creation seed the baseline with only the startup-applied subset: `model`, `thinking`, and `cwd`. Persisted runtime options remain intact for future resume/recovery.

## OpenClaw guard/gate review
- AGENTS / nested AGENTS checked: source path stays within ACP control-plane ownership; no channel or user-facing message rule change.
- Native schema/config/API contract checked: `ensureSession` startup fields are `model`, `thinking`, `cwd`; post-start controls still flow through `session/set_mode` / `session/set_config_option`.
- Runtime/session/security boundary checked: permission profiles and timeouts are no longer suppressed by a broad startup-applied signature.
- Upgrade/backward compatibility checked: runtime-option command updates now preserve unchanged startup-applied baseline entries while dropping changed controls so they are reapplied; resume/recovery now follows the same startup-applied subset rule as initial session creation.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Managed OpenCode ACP sessions could fail on first turn because startup `model`/`thinking` were resent through generic `session/set_config_option`, while a broad cache signature could also skip post-start permission/timeout/mode controls.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source-tree live gateway on macOS using the real OpenCode binary at `$HOME/.opencode/bin/opencode`, ACP backend `acpx`, synthetic Slack DM binding, parent model `openai/gpt-5.4`, and live OpenCode ACP transcript verification.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```text
  OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_AGENT=opencode \
    OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_REQUIRE_TRANSCRIPT=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_IMAGE_PROBE=0 \
    OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_REQUIRE_CRON=0 OPENCLAW_LIVE_ACP_BIND_AGENT_COMMAND_OPENCODE="$HOME/.opencode/bin/opencode" \
    timeout 600s pnpm test:live src/gateway/gateway-acp-bind.live.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live terminal output from the run:

  ```text
  [live-acp-bind] bind command completed
  [live-acp-bind] binding announced for session agent:opencode:acp:<redacted>
  [live-acp-bind] follow-up turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] memory recall turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound session transcript retained the previous token
  [live-acp-bind] memory marker turn completed (attempt 1)
  [live-acp-bind] bound session transcript contains the final marker token
  [live-acp-bind] skipping image probe for opencode
  [live-acp-bind] cron mcp turn <redacted> failed; continuing after bind/image verification
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  6 passed (6)
  Duration  326.90s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The managed OpenCode ACP bind completed, the first follow-up turn completed, the bound ACP transcript retained the previous token, and the final marker token appeared in the bound session transcript. The live test exited 0.
- **What was not tested:** Optional cron MCP creation was not required for this PR and timed out in the live run; the test continued after bind/transcript verification. Broader CI platform/build lanes are left to GitHub Actions.

## Proof
### Automated
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.initialize-session.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-config.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/runtime-options.test.ts`
  - result: 3 files passed across the unit-fast/acp shards; 35 tests passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - result: exit 0.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
  - result: exit 0.

### Regression coverage added/updated
- startup `model` + `thinking` are passed to `ensureSession` and are not resent via `session/set_config_option` on the first turn;
- startup `model` + `thinking` combined with `permissionProfile` + `timeoutSeconds` still applies `approval_policy` and `timeout` before the first turn;
- startup `model` + `thinking` followed by a later runtime-option command update preserves the startup baseline and applies only the new permission/timeout controls before the next turn;
- resumed/persisted runtime options use the same startup baseline for `model`/`thinking` while still applying mode, permission, timeout, and advertised aliases;
- rejected required post-start controls still fail before the turn;
- optional timeout rejection still continues as before.

## Risks and counterexamples
- `permissionProfile` / security boundary: covered by tests proving `approval_policy` still applies when startup model/thinking are already baseline-applied.
- `timeoutSeconds` / compatibility: covered by tests proving `timeout` still applies before the first turn and optional timeout rejection remains non-fatal.
- resume/recovery: covered by updated runtime-config expectations so resumed handles no longer resend startup model/thinking but still apply post-start controls.
- backend advertised aliases: covered by updated alias tests for `permissions` and `timeout_seconds`; startup thinking is not remapped to a generic post-start `effort` config after it has already gone through `ensureSession`.

## Remaining gaps
- None known for the original managed OpenCode first-turn path or the command-update baseline path. The command-update path is covered by focused regression tests rather than a separate live OpenCode run. CI may still exercise broader platform/build lanes.

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`, and unify the cron source-delivery planner into a single shared helper used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. **Unified planner**: Extracted `resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` as the single canonical cron source-delivery planner in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`. Both the normal cron execution path (`run.ts`) and the version-skew fallback path (`run-executor.ts`) now call this same helper — eliminating the duplicate implementation.
2. The helper calls `resolveCronDeliveryPlan(job)` to determine the delivery mode from the job config, then delegates to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` with parameters matching **current main's semantics**:
   - **webhook**: `owner: "none"`, message tool **disabled**, no direct fallback
   - **none**: `owner: "none"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, no direct fallback
   - **announce**: `owner: "direct_fallback"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, `directDelivery: true`, `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from resolved delivery status
3. `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` is a thin wrapper that derives the `CronDeliveryPlan` from the job and calls the shared helper
4. **Removed all legacy field sniffing** — no `toolPolicy`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, or `messageChannel` reads from stale callers. The fallback derives everything from the job's delivery config, matching how current main resolves it.
5. Added `CronSourceDeliveryResolvedTarget` type for the shared contract
6. Added `logWarn` diagnostic when fallback activates

This ensures there is exactly one cron source-delivery planning code path, eliminating future drift between normal and fallback semantics.

## Tests

20 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` unit tests (4 tests):**
1. `delivery.mode "none"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_none"`, message tool enabled but not forced, no direct fallback
2. `delivery.mode "announce"` → `owner: "direct_fallback"`, `reason: "cron_announce"`, direct fallback enabled, `skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from `resolvedDelivery.ok`
3. `delivery.mode "webhook"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_webhook"`, message tool **disabled**, empty target
4. Isolated agentTurn with no delivery config → defaults to announce behavior

**`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` parity tests (4 tests):**
5. `none` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
6. `announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
7. `webhook` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
8. `implicit announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (4 tests):**
9. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "none"` → falls back with `messageToolForced: false`, `owner: "none"`
10. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "announce"` → `direct_fallback` semantics, `forceMessageTool: false`
11. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "webhook"` → message tool disabled
12. No delivery config on isolated agentTurn → announce behavior fallback

**`executeCronRun` level (4 tests):**
13. Stale legacy params (`sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy.forceMessageTool`) → **ignored**, uses job delivery config instead
14. Valid `sourceDelivery` → pass-through, no fallback (regression)
15. Webhook mode → `disableMessageTool: true`, `forceMessageTool: false`
16. Announce mode → `direct_fallback` defaults, `disableMessageTool: false`, `forceMessageTool: false`

## Real behavior proof (current head)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Issue #85051 — isolated cron executor crashed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')` when a version-skew/stale caller invoked the executor without a `sourceDelivery` argument. This branch guards that path via the shared planner `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` (reconstructing a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` from job delivery config + `resolvedDelivery`), and preserves duplicate-announce suppression: in announce mode, when `resolvedDelivery.ok` is missing/undefined it defaults to `true` so `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` stays on (no double-post).

- **Real environment tested**: Checkout commit `8c684df354ac9cff91e0197d29787e3ed9534b31` (branch `fix/cron-isolated-source-delivery-guard`). Primary clean-room run inside Docker `node:24-bookworm` (Node `v24.16.0`, Linux `aarch64`, fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` inside the container with host `node_modules` shadowed by an anonymous volume so Linux-native binaries are used). Cross-checked on host: macOS 15 (Darwin 24.3.0) arm64, Node `v24.10.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. Harness runs `tsx` against the LIVE `src/` source (no mocks of the planner).

- **Failing proof before fix**: The pre-guard upstream/main shape is the raw dereference at `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts:182` on `upstream/main`:

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | sed -n '182p'
    const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;
  ```

  `upstream/main` has no `source-delivery-fallback.ts` (the guard does not exist there):

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts
  fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts' exists on disk, but not in 'upstream/main'
  ```

  The harness reproduces that exact dereference against a stale-caller params object that omits `sourceDelivery`. It throws the issue's exact error (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   A. STALE-CALLER CRASH (pre-guard upstream/main dereference)
  ============================================================
  thrown: true
  error.name: TypeError
  error.message: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
  PASS: pre-guard deref throws TypeError on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: error message matches "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')"
  ```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  ```
  # Docker (primary, clean room)
  docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/repo -v /repo/node_modules -w /repo \
    -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node:24-bookworm bash -lc '
      corepack enable
      corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts'

  # Host (cross-check)
  node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Guarded reconstruction for `announce` / `none` / `webhook` with `sourceDelivery` OMITTED — no crash, valid plans rebuilt (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   B. GUARDED reconstruction (this branch, sourceDelivery OMITTED)
  ============================================================
  announce plan: {
    "owner": "direct_fallback",
    "reason": "cron_announce",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1" },
    "normalFinal": "visible",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": true, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": true, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: announce: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: announce: owner === direct_fallback
  PASS: announce: reason === cron_announce
  PASS: announce: fallback.directDelivery === true
  PASS: announce: target rebuilt from resolvedDelivery
  none plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_none",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1", "accountId": "acct-1", "threadId": "thread-1" },
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: none: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: none: owner === none
  PASS: none: reason === cron_none
  PASS: none: messageTool.enabled === true
  PASS: none: messageTool.force === false
  PASS: none: fallback.directDelivery === false
  webhook plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_webhook",
    "target": {},
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": false, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": false },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: webhook: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: webhook: owner === none
  PASS: webhook: reason === cron_webhook
  PASS: webhook: messageTool.enabled === false
  PASS: webhook: target === {}
  ```

  Duplicate-suppression behavior across `resolvedDelivery.ok` variants, plus normal-path/fallback parity:

  ```
  ============================================================
   C. DUP-SUPPRESSION (announce; resolvedDelivery.ok variants)
  ============================================================
  ok=MISSING -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok MISSING -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true (suppression preserved)
  ok=false   -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: false
  PASS: ok=false -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === false
  ok=true    -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok=true -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true
  PASS: normal-path planner matches fallback wrapper when ok missing

  ============================================================
   RESULT: 22 passed, 0 failed
  ============================================================
  ```

  Existing regression suites (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  === vitest: source-delivery-guard ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (17 tests) 15ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  17 passed (17)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.41s

  === vitest: double-announce ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts (68 tests) 218ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  68 passed (68)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.96s
  ```

  Host cross-check (macOS arm64, Node v24.10.0) — same results: harness `22 passed, 0 failed`; vitest `17 passed (17)` and `68 passed (68)`.

- **Observed result after fix**: With `sourceDelivery` omitted (the version-skew stale-caller path), the pre-guard upstream deref throws the exact `TypeError` from issue #85051, while this branch's `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` reconstructs a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` for every delivery mode (`announce` → `direct_fallback`/`cron_announce`, `none` → `none`/`cron_none`, `webhook` → `none`/`cron_webhook`) without crashing. Duplicate-announce suppression is preserved precisely as designed: missing `resolvedDelivery.ok` → `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true` (suppression on, so a message-tool send already delivered to the target does NOT trigger a second direct-fallback post); `ok:false` → `false`; `ok:true` → `true`. The normal-path planner (`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run.ts`) and the fallback wrapper (`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run-executor.ts`) produce identical plans for the missing-`ok` case, confirming both seams share one policy.

- **What was not tested**: No live Telegram/Discord/transport round-trip was exercised; there is no real message bus in this harness. The proof exercises the planner + executor source-delivery boundary directly at the LIVE source level (the function whose contract the guard protects), plus the colocated Vitest suites that cover the executor wiring (`createCronPromptExecutor` / `executeCronRun`) and the double-announce timer fallback. End-to-end delivery dispatch through an actual channel adapter, and the actual cross-version skew between a stale compiled caller and the new executor in a deployed gateway, were not reproduced.


Fixes #85051

commands.command: requested review: i am moving the complete evidence packet back to this canonical issue after #90957 was closed as a duplicate/superseded evidence packet. related duplicate evidence packet: ```text #90957 — fix(update): require managed service supervision proof after package refresh https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90957 ``` clawsweeper's #90957 review explicitly pointed back here as canonical and noted that the current main still has the updater supervision gap. ## summary `openclaw update` can report success when the updated gateway process on the port is healthy but the managed systemd service unit is no longer installed/loaded/running. this leaves a surviving gateway process that answers probes, while the supervisor state needed for restart persistence is missing. ## why this matters for package-manager updates, the update path can refresh the service environment with `gateway install --force`, then use the expected-version gateway probe as a success shortcut. if an updated gateway process survives on the port but the service manager no longer proves supervision, the update can skip the failure path and leave the host in a fragile state: the currently running process may look healthy, but a later reboot/restart can lose the gateway because the service unit is missing or not loaded. ## latest baseline checked ```text upstream/main: 69a406118cbbd96364bc3ab20031870a7e538d62 ``` i checked that this baseline does not contain the service-supervision proof helper/failure path proposed below. ## source-level reproduction i added a regression case that simulates this sequence: 1. package update refreshes the managed service environment with `gateway install --force`. 2. the gateway port answers with the expected updated version. 3. a later service-state read reports that the managed service is not installed/loaded and the runtime detail says the unit is missing. 4. current `main` still treats the healthy expected-version gateway probe as success, so the update does not call the failure path. regression test name: ```text src/cli/update-cli.test.ts fails package update restart verification when a surviving gateway is healthy but supervisor state is missing ``` ## before-fix github actions evidence test-only branch commit: ```text 7b3f1af69218650463eae297c3d90721f55cc1b4 ``` github actions run: ```text https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27064784784 ``` relevant failing job: ```text checks-node-agentic-cli src/cli/update-cli.test.ts > update-cli > fails package update restart verification when a surviving gateway is healthy but supervisor state is missing assertionerror: expected "vi.fn()" to be called with arguments: [ 1 ] number of calls: 0 ``` interpretation: the reproduced post-refresh state should make `openclaw update` fail, but current `main` does not call `exit(1)` because the expected-version gateway probe is healthy. ## prepared fix branch ```text snowzlm/openclaw:fix/90930-update-systemd-supervision ``` after rebasing onto latest tested `main`: ```text branch head: abe9a1dfabc01214ef66b591d85c54f2c7196ce6 base: 69a406118cbbd96364bc3ab20031870a7e538d62 ``` touched files: ```text src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts ``` ## proposed fix shape the prepared branch keeps the repair in the update restart verification path rather than rewriting systemd unit rendering behavior. for package-manager updates that refresh an existing managed service, success now requires both: 1. expected-version gateway health on the configured port; and 2. verified service supervision state: - `installed === true` - `loaded === true` - `running === true` - no `runtime.missingunit` - no `runtime.missingsupervision` if the gateway port is healthy but service supervision is not verified, update does not silently succeed. it emits diagnostics such as: ```text gateway service supervision was not verified after restart. service state: installed=false, loaded=false, running=false. service runtime: unit openclaw-gateway.service could not be found. ``` the existing shortcut: ```text gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart. ``` only applies when supervision proof also passes. ## after-fix local verification after rebase to latest `main`, focused local checks pass: ```text node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts -t "surviving gateway is healthy but supervisor state is missing" --testtimeout=70000 # 1 passed ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts # passed ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts # 0 warnings, 0 errors ``` ## after-fix github actions evidence rebased branch run: ```text https://github.com/snowzlm/openclaw/actions/runs/27066618320 ``` relevant lanes: ```text checks-node-core-runtime-infra-cli-ui: success checks-node-agentic-cli: success ``` the full fork workflow has one unrelated failure: ```text checks-node-agentic-gateway-methods: failure ``` that failed lane is outside this branch's touched files and outside the update-cli/service-supervision path. the update-cli regression coverage is in the passing cli lanes above. ## expected behavior when a package update sees a healthy updated gateway process but cannot verify that the intended managed service unit is installed, loaded, and running, `openclaw update` should not report success. it should fail or continue the managed restart path with explicit service-supervision diagnostics. ## actual behavior on current main the expected-version gateway probe can be accepted as sufficient, so update can skip the failure path even when the service-manager state is missing. ## notes / boundaries - this is source-reproducible and github actions-reproduced via a focused lifecycle test. - i have not run a credentialed live systemd/wsl repro on a maintainer-owned host. - the prepared branch is intentionally conservative: it verifies service supervision before declaring success; it does not change generated systemd unit rendering/provenance behavior. - i am still not opening a pr while `clawsweeper:no-new-fix-pr`, `clawsweeper:needs-live-repro`, and `clawsweeper:needs-product-decision` remain on this canonical issue. /review

target.title: [Bug]: openclaw update removes systemd service file and skips reinstall when gateway process survives
target.body:
### Bug type
Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker
No

### Summary
`openclaw update` stops the systemd service, updates the package, then skips `gateway install` because the gateway process survives as a direct child — leaving no systemd unit file, so OpenClaw won't survive a reboot.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Install OpenClaw with systemd service via `openclaw gateway install --port 18789` (system-level, not `--user`).
2. Run `openclaw update` from any path (including via the gateway's own `systemd-run` transient unit).
3. Observe: update stops the systemd service, applies the package swap, then reports:
   ```
   Restarting service...
   Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
   ```
4. After update: `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service` shows unit not found:
   ```
   $ systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service
   Unit openclaw-gateway.service could not be found.
   
   $ find /etc/systemd/system -name '*openclaw*'
   (empty)
   
   $ systemctl list-unit-files | grep openclaw
   (empty)
   
   $ pgrep -af "openclaw.*gateway"
   543032 /usr/bin/node /home/fermin/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
   ```

### Expected behavior
After a successful update, the gateway should be managed by the same supervisor (systemd) as before the update — the unit file should exist, the service should be enabled, and `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service` should show active/running. The update should not silently remove systemd lifecycle management.

### Actual behavior
The `Restarting service...` phase detects the gateway process is still alive and reporting the new version, then skips `gateway install` entirely. The systemd unit file is gone, the service is no longer registered, and the gateway runs as an orphan bare process (`/usr/bin/node .../openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`). It works immediately after update but will not survive a reboot or crash.

### OpenClaw version
2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

### Operating system
WSL2 (Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2, x64)

### Install method
npm global

### Model
openai/gpt-5.5 (default)

### Provider / routing chain
openclaw -> (default runtime)

### Additional provider/model setup details
N/A — the bug is in the update/service lifecycle, not model routing.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence
Full update log:

```
Current Node (/home/fermin/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin/node) differs from the managed gateway service Node (/usr/bin/node).
Using the managed service Node for this update so the gateway can start after the upgrade.
Updating OpenClaw...

Stopping managed gateway service before package update...
Stopped systemd service: openclaw-gateway.service

Update Result: OK
  Root: /home/fermin/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  Before: 2026.5.20
  After: 2026.6.1

Steps:
  ✓ global update (8.59s)
  ✓ global install swap (2.72s)
  ✓ openclaw doctor (10.89s)

Total time: 33.67s

...

Restarting service...
Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
Gateway: restarted and verified.
I've evolved. Try to keep up.
```

After the update, no systemd unit file exists:
- `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` — missing
- `systemctl list-unit-files | grep openclaw` — empty
- Gateway process alive as orphan bare process (PID 543032)

The process survived because it was a child of the update's own `systemd-run` transient unit (`openclaw-update-20260606053649.service`), which kept it alive while the managed gateway service was stopped.

### Impact and severity
Affected: All Linux/WSL2 users with systemd-managed gateway services who run `openclaw update`.
Severity: High (silently removes lifecycle management).
Frequency: Reproduced on 2026.6.1 update from 2026.5.20, likely affects all updates with the "Gateway already reports" short-circuit.
Consequence: OpenClaw stops working after reboot or any service disruption, with no indication until the system comes back up.

### Additional information
Related to #41914 (closed as COMPLETED, but the fix apparently didn't cover this variant or regressed). This variant differs from the original report (#41914) in that the gateway process **survives** the service stop (as a direct child of the update's `systemd-run` transient unit), which triggers the `"Gateway already reports"` guard and bypasses the `gateway install` step entirely.

The ClawSweeper code review confirmed the root cause in current main:
- [`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:2121`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts#L2121): post-refresh health probe sets `refreshedGatewayAlreadyHealthy = true` when gateway reports expected version
- [`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:1202`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts#L1202): service refresh runs `gateway install --force` but success is driven by health probe, not by proving supervisor-owned unit exists
- [`src/cli/update-cli.test.ts:5060`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli.test.ts#L5060): existing test covers the shortcut but not the missing-unit variant

Workaround: Run `openclaw gateway install --force --port 18789` from a terminal outside the gateway's process tree.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`externalAbort: false` in trajectory). The existing code treated *any* AbortError as a user/gateway cancellation, causing a silent failure chain in group/channel sessions.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `externalAbort: false`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = true (name === "AbortError"), `normalizedFailover` = null → **throws**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: not a user/restart/draining abort → `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```
**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## After-fix behavior proof

Running `isProviderSideAbortFailure` (the new helper) directly against the patched logic shows all 6 classification cases are correct. Output from `node /tmp/patch-verify.mjs` on the patched branch (logic inlined from `agent-runner-execution.ts`, no provider/user data):

```
=== PR #90908 after-fix behavior proof ===

isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOp) → true = visible error, false = SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN

✅ Raw AbortError, no external signal (provider-side abort — the bug scenario)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — all attempts abort-like (multi-model, all provider-side aborts)
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ FallbackSummaryError — ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT, no abort text) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — mixed (abort + rate_limit) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ Raw AbortError with upstream abort signal fired (real cancellation — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ Raw AbortError with result.kind=aborted (user /stop — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

Result: 6 passed, 0 failed
```

Key: the only case that changes from `SILENT` → `visible-error` is the bug scenario (FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeouts). Ordinary timeouts, rate-limit, mixed failures, and real cancellations all preserve `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`.

## Fix (4 commits — all ClawSweeper/Codex issues addressed)

### Part 1 — `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`
Gate `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:
```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
```
→ With fallback configured: provider-side AbortError enters fallback loop.

### Part 2 — `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
New `isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOperation)` helper covering two shapes:

1. **Raw AbortError** (single-model/no-fallback — `throwFallbackFailureSummary` re-throws when `attempts.length <= 1`)
2. **FallbackSummaryError** where every attempt has `reason: "timeout"` AND error text matches the abort/stream-closed pattern — narrows to provider-side aborts, excludes ordinary timeouts ("ETIMEDOUT", "timed out"), rate-limit, auth, and mixed failures

Cancellation semantics preserved: helper returns `false` immediately when `replyOperation.result?.kind === "aborted"` (set by `abortByUser`/`abortForRestart`) or `replyOperation.abortSignal?.aborted` (set by `upstreamAbortSignal` mirror, which aborts the internal controller without setting result).

## Tests (213 agent-runner-execution + 78 model-fallback, all pass)

New tests added (18 cases across 3 × it.each(NON_DIRECT_FAILURE_SURFACE_CASES)):
- **FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeout → visible error** (regression #90908 multi-model path)
- **FallbackSummaryError ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT) → SILENT** (ClawSweeper P2 regression)
- **FallbackSummaryError mixed reasons → SILENT** (rate_limit + timeout keeps silence)
- **upstream abort signal fired → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)
- **user /stop abort → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.

Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: **Yes** — M3 requests now actually return content; M2.x behavior is unchanged.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: **No**.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: **No**.
- Highest-risk area: the model-id regex matching an unexpected MiniMax id family. Mitigated by anchoring to `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` so only `MiniMax-M3*` prefixes match, with regression tests for both `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: clawsweeper's docs-alignment finding (added the M3 exception to `docs/providers/minimax.md` Warning + Thinking-defaults accordion, `docs/tools/thinking.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`).
